name: Dashboard — open the app and read where you stand
summary: The complete journey of opening the home screen and reading your money — the headline number, what's safe to spend, the payday countdown, net worth — and the rungs that render wrong or disagree across surfaces.

steps.title: Open the dashboard
steps.does: You land on the home screen (or tap the Dash tab) and the whole view paints.
steps.plain: The starting screen. In Postman terms goPage is the request that opens the collection; its last line calls renderAll() — the composite-API that assembles the entire dashboard view in one pass.

steps.title: Refresh the model everything reads from
steps.does: Before painting, renderAll recomputes the shared financial model so every tile reads the same snapshot.
steps.plain: The composite-API first calls one upstream endpoint (refreshModel) so the tiles below all read MODEL — the same assembled response — instead of each re-querying entity fields and drifting apart.

steps.title: Read live balance
steps.does: Reads the true spendable cash — BRAIN.balance.get(), falling back to S.bal.
steps.plain: Reading the canonical balance field. getLiveBal asks the BRAIN.balance bubble for the number rather than touching S.bal raw — the single source of truth for cash.

steps.title: Cash hero renders the headline number
steps.does: The big number paints — Safe-to-spend headroom from the plan snapshot (falls back to live balance). Counter-rolls each time the value changes.
steps.plain: The headline composite-API writes the number into #h-bal. Each render diffs the old value against the new and, if they differ by >= 0.5, tweens between them frame-by-frame — so the headline number visibly slews across frames instead of snapping to its true value. That render-drift is the headline-instability defect.

steps.title: Counter-roll animation tweens the digits
steps.does: Drives the per-frame digit roll on the hero (and NW / Max-per-day) over ~380ms with an ease-out curve.
steps.plain: The animator the hero hands its from/to to. It paints intermediate dollar values (from + (to-from)*eased) into the element on every requestAnimationFrame tick — so for ~380ms the headline shows numbers that were never the real balance. This is the mechanism behind the SLY-4 render-drift.

steps.title: Receipt sub-line shows the math
steps.does: Under the hero: 'cash − $X bills − $Y living = $Z' so the headroom is explainable.
steps.plain: The trust sub-line. It's built with fmt() (rounds to whole dollars) and painted in low-contrast greys (rcpt-bills / rcpt-living on --text3) on the dark hero panel. Small grey-on-grey numbers are hard to read on the S23, and rounded/empty values can render malformed ($#0-style) — the legibility defect.

steps.title: MAX PER DAY / daily budget tile
steps.does: Shows what's safe to spend the rest of today, tinted green/amber/red by severity.
steps.plain: The daily-budget tile reads MODEL.safeToSpendToday — assembled from getDynamicDailyBudget() (index.html:2991): available cash / days-to-payday, capped at your weekday/weekend cap, minus what you've already spent today. Healthy here.

steps.title: Payday countdown bar + label
steps.does: Fills the cycle-progress bar and prints 'N days to payday' under the hero.
steps.plain: The dashboard countdown reads MODEL.daysToPayday (set at index.html:6822). Other surfaces compute the same countdown inline with daysLeft(S.payday) (index.html:2942), and the two diverge on Feb-31-style month clamping and payday-morning gating — so the same 'days to payday' shows different numbers across tabs. Cross-surface incoherence (FR-06).

steps.title: Liquid net-worth tile
steps.does: Shows '+$X liquid net worth · tap for full picture'; hidden on a coral (over-budget) day so it doesn't soften urgency.
steps.plain: The net-worth strip reads MODEL.liquidNet (assets minus liabilities you could access today) and counter-rolls it like the hero. On a negative-headroom day it's hidden so the red hero stands alone. Healthy.

steps.title: Goal / debt mini-tiles render
steps.does: Immediate Debts grid and Recent Spending list paint below the hero.
steps.plain: The supporting tiles: the debts grid lists immediate debts and renderDashTxns paints the last few transactions. Each is a small composite read off S — no writes, just the view.

steps.title: Tap a number → explainer modal
steps.does: Tapping a tile opens its math: Max-per-day → breakdown; hero → balance edit; NW strip → full picture.
steps.plain: Every number is tappable to its 'why'. explainMaxPerDay (3221) re-derives the same available/days/cap/spent math and shows the receipt; openHeroBalEdit (7771) lets you correct the balance; openNetWorthModal (7620) shows the asset/liability breakdown. The explainer rung works.
